mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of rate-hiking months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer prices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate output capacity, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns specifically during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when FEDFUNDS is rising, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window or positive returns occur in < 50% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing below 500 thousand units signals a contraction in the construction sector and broader credit tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when HOUST drops below 500.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside stagnant or falling federal funds rates indicate real rate compression and loss of central bank credibility, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS remains flat, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter exclusively during periods of low market volatility.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when VIX exceeds 20, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of high-GDP quarters.
